requirements.evidence: select_top_k(candidates, *, k=DEFAULT_K=3) matches acceptance criteria verbatim ('k configurable parameter defaulting to 3'). Test spec's exact k=1,3,5 fixture-list assertions implemented in test_topic_selector.py and pass.

architecture_conformance.evidence: docs/LLD/query-agent.md's topic_selector.py section (top-k selection, k default 3, SearchCandidates input) matches implementation. TopicCandidate field names/types (file_id:uint64, path:string, score:float) verified byte-for-byte against proto/hivemind.proto's CandidateTopic message. Decoupled dataclass approach mirrors agents/ingestion/shortlist.py precedent as claimed (verified: shortlist.py independently defines its own TopicCandidate too). Comment: unlike shortlist.py, which ships a GrpcSearchCandidatesClient adapter translating the real proto CandidateTopic -> its dataclass, topic_selector.py declares the SearchCandidatesFn type alias but no analogous adapter/wiring.py exists in agents/query/ (confirmed via ls). Whoever wires issue #25's SearchCandidates RPC call must write that translation themselves. Given identical field names/types this is a cheap, low-risk adapter (not a hidden landmine), and the gap is explicitly disclosed in the module docstring rather than silently deferred, but it is a real, non-zero integration task that composes slightly worse with #25 than shortlist.py's fuller precedent did.

regression_risk.evidence: cd agents && python3 -m pytest query/ -q -> 29 passed. Full suite (excluding known-excluded test_e2e_smoke.py): 240 passed, 2 failed. The 2 failures are ingestion/test_shortlist.py::test_grpc_client_translates_request_response and test_grpc_client_is_a_valid_search_candidates_fn_for_shortlist, both google.protobuf.runtime_version.VersionError (gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6) -- a pre-existing environment/dependency issue unrelated to this diff, same class as documented issue #46, not touched by topic_selector.py. ruff check agents/query/ -> All checks passed.

edge_cases.evidence: k=0 -> [] (explicit early return, tested). Negative k -> raises ValueError (tested); acceptance criteria is silent on negative k but raising is a safe, defensive choice, not tested against pre-existing test_shortlist.py which uses the same convention for top_k/pool_size. k > pool size -> returns all candidates in ranked order (tested). Empty candidate pool -> [] regardless of k (tested). Duplicate/tied scores -> deterministic stable tie-break via sort key (-score, original_index), verified by dedicated test with 3 tied 0.5-score entries preserving original relative order (10,12,13) after 0.9-score entry.

security.evidence: No I/O, no external input trust boundary, no injection surface; pure in-memory list transform.

performance.evidence: O(n log n) sort over the candidate pool; pool size is caller-bounded (SearchCandidatesRequest.max_results). No unbounded work.

maintainability.evidence: Extensive docstring disclosing every design choice and its rationale (decoupled dataclass, free function vs class, DEFAULT_K constant, deferred RPC wiring). DEFAULT_K exported for reuse by 4.4.2/4.4.3 as claimed.

test_coverage.evidence: Covers literal test spec (k=1,3,5), default-k, full-ordering, k=0, k>pool, empty pool, negative k, and tie-break determinism -- exceeds the literal spec.

confidence.evidence: Implementation, tests, LLD, and proto were independently read and cross-checked line-by-line; no discrepancy found between docstring claims and actual repo state (verified absence of agents/query/wiring.py, verified shortlist.py's adapter pattern, verified proto field shapes).

possible_improvements: Consider adding a GrpcSearchCandidatesClient-style adapter (or at least a stub) in agents/query/ now, mirroring shortlist.py's pattern, so issue #25 has a ready translation layer rather than needing to rediscover the pattern.

Document explicitly in topic_selector.py's docstring what happens on negative k (currently only inferable from the ValueError message) as part of its public contract, since the acceptance criteria doesn't specify this.
non_blocking_findings: No GrpcSearchCandidatesFn adapter/wiring exists yet in agents/query/, unlike ingestion/shortlist.py's precedent; low-cost but real adapter work deferred to issue #25.
Pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch causes 2 unrelated test failures in ingestion/test_shortlist.py (same class as issue #46); does not affect this subtask.
